Quality Education.reason: The course is tertiary education (Target 4.3) and aims to develop analytical, research, and writing skills (Target 4.4). It also fosters understanding of historical governance and inequality, contributing to knowledge for sustainable development (Target 4.7).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course explicitly focuses on 'colonial integration and differentiation' and 'heterogeneous legal structures,' directly analyzing historical discriminatory laws and policies that created inequalities (Targets 10.2, 10.3).

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course's core focus on 'Japanese Colonial Empire,' 'colonial rule,' and 'legal aspects' (法制層面, 異法域構造) directly examines the formation and function of institutions and justice systems under colonial governance (Targets 16.3, 16.6).
